title: Async media processing: thumbnail generation, HEIC conversion, dimension extraction
description:
In the spawned processing task: (1) If HEIC: convert to JPEG using image crate with heic feature. (2) Load image with image crate, extract dimensions (width, height). (3) Generate thumbnail at max 400x400 preserving aspect ratio using fast_image_resize (Lanczos3 filter). Store thumbnail as {id}_thumb.jpg at JPEG quality 85. (4) Update media row: file_path, mime_type, file_size, width, height. Processing state: media row initially has blurhash=NULL and width=NULL as 'still processing' sentinel.

AGENT WORKFLOW: Spawn a research subagent to study fast_image_resize API and Lanczos3 resampling setup. Spawn a testing subagent to generate test images of known dimensions and verify output dimensions exactly.

TEST DISCIPLINE:
- 800x600 image → thumbnail is exactly 400x300 (preserves aspect, fits in 400x400)
- 400x800 image → thumbnail is 200x400 (portrait orientation handled)
- 400x400 image → thumbnail is exactly 400x400 (no upscaling needed)
- 100x100 image → thumbnail is 100x100 (do NOT upscale small images)
- Verify output dimensions using imagesize crate or image::open() — NOT our own resize code
- HEIC input: verify output is valid JPEG (check magic bytes of output file)
- Processing failure: media row updated with error indicator, GET /api/v1/media/:id returns 'failed' state

DEFENSIVE PROGRAMMING:
- Handle corrupt image files: wrap image::open() in a timeout + catch-unwind equivalent (async-aware)
- Limit processing memory: reject images with pixel count > 50 megapixels before loading
- Processing task must not crash the server on corrupt input — catch panics
acceptance_criteria: Output thumbnail dimensions exactly correct per aspect ratio math; HEIC converted to valid JPEG; corrupt images handled without panic; oversized pixel count rejected
